title: silene vulgaris subsp. marginata
intro: Silene vulgaris subsp. marginata, also known as fire pink or pink catchfly, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the carnation family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Silene vulgaris subsp. marginata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea or using the plant's extracts in ointments and salves to promote skin health and wound healing.
